overview.tagline: Bergen County's most exclusive enclave atop the Palisades
overview.description: Alpine is a small, affluent borough perched atop the Palisades cliffs overlooking the Hudson River. Known as one of the wealthiest communities in the United States, Alpine features sprawling estates on large wooded lots, offering privacy and natural beauty just minutes from Manhattan. The borough is home to a section of the Palisades Interstate Park and has attracted numerous celebrities and business leaders.
overview.elevation: 500 ft (Palisades ridge) to near sea level at river
highlights: One of the wealthiest zip codes in the United States
Home to a major section of Palisades Interstate Park
Dramatic Hudson River and NYC skyline views from the cliffs
Large estate-style properties on multi-acre lots
Rich history dating to early Dutch settlement

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Alpine's large estate lots and unique properties make valuations highly individual.
realEstate.recentTrends: Alpine's luxury real estate market remains robust, with demand driven by buyers seeking privacy, large lots, and proximity to NYC. Teardowns and new construction of modern mansions are common. Properties routinely sell in the multi-million dollar range.
neighborhoodNotes: Closter Dock Road area features many of the borough's largest estates
Properties along the Palisades ridge command premium prices for Hudson River views
The western section near Closter offers slightly more modest homes
Minimum lot sizes help preserve the borough's rural, estate-like character

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly through the borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days are Tuesday and Friday
Trash must be in sealed bags or covered containers
Maximum weight per container is 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly as part of the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags or Styrofoam accepted
Yard waste collection available seasonally

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Suez Water (Veolia). Many properties in Alpine use private septic systems.
details: Water bills are issued quarterly
Many homes rely on septic systems rather than municipal sewer
Septic systems must be inspected and maintained per borough code
Contact Veolia for water service issues

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Alpine Building Department handles all construction and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for all construction, renovation, and tree removal
Strict zoning regulations protect the borough's residential character
Environmental permits may be required near Palisades cliffs
Applications submitted at Borough Hall

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Alpine is a residential community with limited commercial parking needs.
details: No municipal parking lots or meters
On-street parking restrictions apply on certain roads
Palisades Interstate Park has its own parking areas
Commuter parking is available in neighboring Closter

additionalServices.title: Leaf & Brush Collection
additionalServices.description: Seasonal collection of leaves and brush by the DPW.
details: Fall leaf collection runs October through December
Brush must be cut to 4-foot lengths and bundled
No contractor debris accepted in municipal pickup

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Alpine enforces noise regulations to maintain the borough's quiet, residential character.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 8:00 AM and after 6:00 PM weekdays
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers restricted to certain hours
Excessive noise from any source subject to complaint-based enforcement

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their lots in accordance with borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and not exceed 10 inches in height
Dead or hazardous trees must be removed promptly
Driveways and walkways must be kept in good repair
No storage of junk vehicles or debris on residential properties

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed and controlled at all times.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed annually with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated to maintain safety on Alpine's narrow roads.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets without permission
No parking on roadways during snow emergencies
Commercial vehicles over a certain weight restricted from residential streets
Violators subject to ticketing and towing

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing and zoning codes.
keyRules: Rental units must pass inspection before occupancy
Short-term rentals (Airbnb-style) are not permitted in residential zones
Landlords responsible for tenant compliance with borough ordinances
Certificate of Habitability required

other.title: Tree Removal
other.summary: Alpine has strict tree preservation ordinances given its wooded character.
keyRules: Permit required to remove any tree over 6 inches in diameter
Replacement trees may be required for removed specimens
Fines for unauthorized tree removal can be substantial
Tree removal near the Palisades cliffs requires additional environmental review

publicSchools.name: Alpine Public School
publicSchools.notes: Small school serving the borough's elementary and middle school students. High school students attend Tenafly High School through a sending-receiving relationship.

privateSchools.name: The Elisabeth Morrow School
privateSchools.notes: Located in nearby Englewood, popular with Alpine families

nearbyHigherEd: Columbia University (Manhattan, accessible via Palisades Interstate Parkway)

whyMoveHere: Ultra-private estate living on large wooded lots
Spectacular Hudson River and NYC skyline views
Close proximity to Manhattan despite rural feel
Access to Palisades Interstate Park for hiking and recreation
Highly safe community with very low crime rates

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: Car via Palisades Interstate Parkway to GWB or bus
commuteToNYC.notes: Most residents commute by car. The Palisades Interstate Parkway provides direct access to the George Washington Bridge. NJ Transit bus service is available along Route 9W.

costOfLiving.summary: Alpine has one of the highest costs of living in New Jersey, driven primarily by real estate values and property taxes.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Significantly above the New Jersey average, especially in housing costs
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Well above the national average across all categories

pros: Natural beauty with Palisades cliffs and wooded landscapes
Proximity to NYC with a rural atmosphere
cons: Very high property taxes and home prices
Limited commercial amenities — must travel to neighboring towns for shopping
Small school district with limited extracurricular offerings
Winding roads can be challenging in winter weather

restaurants.name: Barrique Kitchen & Wine Bar
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian-American cuisine in a refined setting, popular with locals.

restaurants.name: Alpine Ristorante
restaurants.description: Classic Italian dining with a warm, neighborhood atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Rio 22
restaurants.description: Brazilian steakhouse and seafood restaurant on Route 9W.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park — Alpine Section
parks.description: Dramatic cliffside park along the Hudson River with hiking trails, scenic overlooks, the Alpine Boat Basin, and picnic areas. Part of the larger Palisades Interstate Park system.

parks.name: Alpine Lookout
parks.description: Scenic overlook offering panoramic views of the Hudson River, Yonkers, and the NYC skyline.

parks.name: Greenbrook Sanctuary
parks.description: A private 165-acre nature preserve with hiking trails, a pond, and diverse wildlife. Membership required.

events.name: Alpine Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food, games, and activities for families at the Alpine firehouse.

events.name: Palisades Interstate Park Events
events.description: Seasonal nature walks, historical programs, and outdoor recreation events organized by the Palisades Interstate Park Commission.

attractions.name: Alpine Boat Basin
attractions.description: Historic riverside recreation area within Palisades Interstate Park offering picnicking, fishing, and boat launches along the Hudson River.

attractions.name: Women's Federation Monument
attractions.description: Stone tower monument at the top of the Palisades, built in 1929 by the New Jersey State Federation of Women's Clubs to help preserve the cliffs.

attractions.name: Rio Vista
attractions.description: The former estate of Manuel Rionda, now part of Palisades Interstate Park, with trails offering river views.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Alpine Borough Hall, 100 Church Street, Alpine, NJ 07620
